scoped_audit.rationale: FastAPI codebase size (2449 files) exceeds benchmark cost cap (50 files per module). Audit scoped to 3 modules where known bugs exist.

exact_matches.description: HTTPDigest.__call__ raises exception even when auto_error=False
exact_matches.evidence: fastapi/security/http.py:L415-L419 — unconditional HTTPException raise, ignores auto_error parameter

exact_matches.description: convert_underscores=False not respected for header Pydantic models
exact_matches.evidence: fastapi/dependencies/utils.py:L766 — getattr default fallback overrides user setting

close_matches.description: Model description field truncated when description contains form feed ( ) character
close_matches.evidence: fastapi/routing.py:L531-L534 — description.split(' ')[0] without distinguishing user-set vs auto-derived
close_matches.note: Subagent correctly identified the root cause (  truncation) but classified it as 'design intent' rather than 'bug' — still a valid detection

false_negatives.description: Unions not properly supported when using Form parameters
false_negatives.why_missed: Requires deep analysis of Form field type handling across Union types. Structural audit (Tier B) may not detect behavioral edge cases in type combinatorics without explicit spec constraints.

false_negatives.description: Annotated custom validators (AfterValidator) not properly supported in query params
false_negatives.why_missed: AfterValidator is a Pydantic-specific feature. ADD's generic subagent does not have domain knowledge of Pydantic validator lifecycle. Would need a security-like domain-specific rules file.

false_negatives.description: Pydantic 2.11 compatibility — internal annotation usage pattern incompatible
false_negatives.why_missed: Version-compatibility bugs require testing against unreleased library versions. Not detectable via static code analysis alone.

fp_examples: OPEN-P3-1: generate_operation_id marked as deprecated — subagent reported this as P3 but it's already documented with DeprecationWarning
SEC-P2-1: API key header name inference — subagent suggested potential issue but existing tests cover this case
DEP-P2-1: analyze_param complexity warning — architectural concern, not a bug

analysis.strengths: ADD detected 3/6 known bugs (KB-1 exact, KB-4 exact, KB-2 close). The HTTPDigest auto_error bug (KB-1) was detected as P0 with precise source-level evidence. Detection quality is strong for bug types involving explicit parameter handling (auto_error, convert_underscores) and string processing (  truncation).
analysis.weaknesses: 3 false negatives (KB-3, KB-5, KB-6) expose structural limitations: (1) Type combinatorics bugs (Union+Form) require deeper semantic analysis than current subagent prompts support, (2) Domain-specific features (AfterValidator) require framework-specific knowledge, (3) Forward-compatibility bugs require runtime testing, not static analysis.
analysis.tier_b_constraint: This benchmark was run at Tier B (structural audit). The 50% recall rate may understate full-audit capability since structural mode uses general engineering principles rather than spec-specific constraints. A Tier A audit with explicit design contracts would likely have higher recall for KB-3 and KB-5.
analysis.scoped_audit_note: This is a scoped audit (3/200+ modules). The full codebase would produce different precision/recall metrics. Scoped audit is per benchmark cost cap (5 subagents max, 50 files max per module).
